title: Donated-compute worker client (consent, resource caps, signed, runs on donor's own machine)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The worker client runs on the donor's own machine with explicit, informed consent, an obvious kill control, and hard resource caps; it collects no PII and no behavioral telemetry, honoring the donated-compute and anti-botnet guardrails. It depends on coord-011.
objective: Build the signed, open donated-compute worker client the donor runs on their own machine with explicit consent, resource caps, and a stop/kill control.
acceptanceCriteria: A signed, open client the donor installs and runs on their own machine with explicit, informed consent and an obvious stop/kill control; honors per-chunk CPU/memory caps.
Submits result + checksum + technical env metadata only; pseudonymous, opt-in attribution; no PII, no behavioral telemetry; no secrets written to logs/artifacts.
output: A PR adding the signed, consent-first donated-compute worker client with resource caps and a kill switch.
